case_title: MARK LIPIAN MD et al VS. BRIAN T. ANDREWS MD et al

docket_entries.description: ASSOCIATION OF ATTORNEYS: MCCOLGAN, KATHLEEN D. ADDED AS ATTORNEY FOR ANDREWS MD, BRIAN T.

DECLARATION OF STEVEN A. FABBRO, ESQ. IN SUPPORT OF PLTF'S MEMO OF P&A IN OPPOS TO DEFT'S FOR ORDER STAYING ACTION AND REQUIRING PARTIES TO ARBITRATE MATTER; POS FILED BY PLAINTIFF LIPIAN MD, MARK LIPIAN, MARY

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT BRIAN ANDREWS PETITION FOR ORDER STAYING ACTION BETWEEN THE ARBITRATING PARTIES ONLY AND REQUIRING PLAINTIFF TO ARBITRATE THE CONTROVERSY. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PETITION FOR ORDER IS GRANTED. BOTH PLAINTIFFS WILL ARBITRATE THEIR CLAIMS. SEE GROSS VS. RECABAREN (1988) 206 CAL.APP. 3RD 771 AND MORMILE VS. SINCLAIR (1994) 21 CAL.APP. 4TH 1508. THIS ACTION IS STAYED PENDING COMPLETION OF THE ARBITRATION. (DEFENSE COUNSEL TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: BRENDA FIELDS, CSR #7235

LAW AND MOTION, 302, PLAINTIFFS MARK LIPIAN, AND, MARY LIPIAN MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED AND AMENDED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: OFF CALENDAR, AS MOOT, IN LIGHT OF THE ORDER GRANTING STAY. THE COURT ADDS: CCP 1281.4 DOES CONTROL. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: BRENDA FIELDS, CSR #7235

Notice Of Petition And Pettion For Order Staying Action Between The Arbitrating Parties Only And Requiring Pltf To Arbitrate The Controversy
Off Calendar Jun-14-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT BRIAN ANDREWS Petition for Order Staying Action Between the Arbitrating Parties Only and Requiring Plaintiff to Arbitrate the Controversy. Appearances: Steven A. Fabbro, Esq. Attorney at Law, (415) 391-6850 appearing for Plaintiffs and opposing party; Susan K. Riggio, Esq. of Galloway Lucchese (925) 930-9090 appearing for Defendant and Moving party; Denise H. Greer, Esq. of Schmid & Voiles (213) 473-8754 appearing for Defendant and Moving party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Petition for Order is granted. Both plaintiffs will arbitrate their claims. See Gross vs. Recabaren (1988) 206 Cal.App. 3rd 771 and Mormile vs. Sinclair (1994) 21 Cal.App. 4th 1508. This action is stayed pending completion of the arbitration. (Defense counsel to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Brenda Fields, CSR# 7235

Ntc Of Mtn For Leave To File First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Jun-14-2010 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFFS MARK LIPIAN, and, MARY LIPIAN Motion for Leave to File First Amended Complaint. Appearances: Steven A. Fabbro, Esq. Attorney at Law, (415) 391-6850 appearing for Plaintiffs and moving party; Susan K. Riggio, Esq. of Galloway Lucchese (925) 930-9090 appearing for Defendant and Opposing party; Denise H. Greer, Esq. of Schmid & Voiles (213) 473-8754 appearing for Defendant and Opposing party. Argued and the Court adopted and amended its tentative ruling as follows: Off Calendar, as moot, in light of the order granting stay. The Court adds: CCP 1281.4 does control. (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Brenda Fields, CSR# 7235

Ntc Of Mtn For Leave To File First Amended Complaint

The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion is continued to June 14, 2010 to be heard with motion to compel arbitration. (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797
